K.2A.desc: Count a set of objects up to at least 20 and demonstrate that the last number said tells the number of objects

K.2B.desc: Read, write, and represent whole numbers from 0 to at least 20

K.2C.desc: Count a set of objects up to at least 20 using concrete and pictorial models

K.2D.desc: Recognize instantly the quantity of a small group of objects in organized and random arrangements

K.2E.desc: Generate a set using concrete and pictorial models that represents a number that is more than, less than, and equal to a given number

K.2F.desc: Generate a number that is one more than or one less than another number

K.2G.desc: Compare sets of objects up to at least 20 using comparative language

K.2H.desc: Use comparative language to describe two numbers up to 20 presented as written numerals

K.2I.desc: Compose and decompose numbers up to 10 using objects, pictures, and numbers

K.3A.desc: Model the action of joining to represent addition and the action of separating to represent subtraction

K.3B.desc: Solve word problems using objects and drawings to find sums up to 10 and differences within 10

K.3C.desc: Explain the strategies used to solve problems involving addition and subtraction

K.6A.desc: Identify two-dimensional shapes, including circles, triangles, rectangles, and squares

K.6B.desc: Identify three-dimensional solids, including cylinders, cones, spheres, and cubes

K.7A.desc: Compare two objects with a common measurable attribute

K.7B.desc: Describe a length by using a number and a unit

K.8A.desc: Collect, sort, and organize data into two or three categories

K.8B.desc: Use data to create real-object graphs, picture graphs, and bar-type graphs

1.2A.desc: Recognize instantly the quantity of structured arrangements

1.2B.desc: Use concrete and pictorial models to compose and decompose numbers up to 120

1.2C.desc: Use objects, pictures, and expanded and standard forms to represent numbers up to 120

1.2D.desc: Generate a number that is 10 more or 10 less than a given number

1.2E.desc: Create two-digit numbers using tens and ones with concrete models and pictures

1.2F.desc: Read and write two-digit numbers using numerals, number words, and expanded form

1.2G.desc: Compare and order whole numbers up to 120 using comparative language and symbols

1.3A.desc: Use concrete and pictorial models to determine the sum of a multiple of 10 and a one-digit number

1.3B.desc: Use objects and pictorial models to solve word problems involving joining, separating, and comparing

1.3C.desc: Use the commutative property of addition to find sums

1.3D.desc: Apply basic fact strategies to add and subtract within 20

1.3E.desc: Identify and write number sentences that match story problems

1.5A.desc: Recite numbers forward and backward from any given number between 1 and 120

1.6A.desc: Compare lengths of objects by using comparative language

1.6B.desc: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units

1.7A.desc: Describe the attributes of two-dimensional shapes using informal and formal language

1.7B.desc: Distinguish between attributes that define a two-dimensional or three-dimensional shape

1.8A.desc: Collect, organize, and record data in more than one way

1.8B.desc: Use data to create real-object graphs, picture graphs, and bar-type graphs

1.8C.desc: Read and interpret data in real-object graphs, picture graphs, and bar-type graphs

2.2A.desc: Use concrete and pictorial models to compose and decompose numbers up to 1,200

2.2B.desc: Use objects, pictures, and numbers to represent place value in the hundreds, tens, and ones

2.2C.desc: Read and write numbers up to 1,200 using numerals, number words, and expanded form

2.2D.desc: Generate a number that is 10 or 100 more or less than a given number

2.2E.desc: Use the number line to compare and order numbers up to 1,200

2.2F.desc: Use concrete models to represent equivalent sets involving fractions with denominators of 2, 3, or 4

2.4A.desc: Recall basic facts to add and subtract within 20 with automaticity

2.4B.desc: Add up to four two-digit numbers and subtract two-digit numbers

2.4C.desc: Solve one-step and multi-step word problems involving addition and subtraction

2.4D.desc: Use the commutative and associative properties to find sums and differences

2.5A.desc: Use repeated addition to find the total number of objects in a set of equal groups

2.5B.desc: Use arrays, area models, and repeated addition to represent multiplication

2.6A.desc: Classify and sort two-dimensional shapes using informal language

2.6B.desc: Classify and sort three-dimensional solids, including spheres, cones, cylinders, rectangular prisms, and cubes

2.7A.desc: Measure the length of objects using appropriate non-standard and standard units

2.7B.desc: Tell and write time to the hour and half hour using analog and digital clocks

2.7C.desc: Identify coins and their values and describe relationships among them

2.8A.desc: Collect, organize, record, and interpret data

2.8B.desc: Construct picture graphs and bar graphs with single-unit scales

3.2A.desc: Compose and decompose numbers up to 100,000 as a sum of so many ten thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones

3.2B.desc: Describe the value of the digits in numbers up to 100,000 using place value

3.2C.desc: Read and write numbers up to 100,000 using numerals, number words, and expanded form

3.2D.desc: Compare and order whole numbers up to 100,000 and represent comparisons using symbols

3.3A.desc: Represent fractions greater than zero and less than or equal to one with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8

3.3B.desc: Determine the corresponding fraction greater than zero and less than or equal to one on a number line

3.3C.desc: Explain that the unit fraction 1/b represents the quantity formed by one part of a whole that has been partitioned into b equal parts

3.3D.desc: Compose and decompose a fraction a/b with a numerator greater than zero and less than or equal to b as a sum of parts 1/b

3.3E.desc: Identify concrete models that represent equivalent fractions

3.3H.desc: Compare two fractions with the same denominator or the same numerator using symbols and justification

3.4A.desc: Solve with fluency one-step and two-step problems involving addition and subtraction within 1,000

3.4B.desc: Round to the nearest 10 or 100 using place value understanding

3.4D.desc: Determine the total number of objects when equally-sized groups of objects are combined or arranged in arrays

3.4E.desc: Represent multiplication facts by using a variety of approaches such as repeated addition, equal-sized groups, arrays

3.4F.desc: Use strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, to multiply a two-digit number by a one-digit number

3.4G.desc: Use the commutative and associative properties of multiplication

3.4H.desc: Determine the number of objects in each group when a set of objects is partitioned into equal shares

3.4I.desc: Model division as equal sharing or repeated subtraction

3.4J.desc: Determine a quotient using the relationship between multiplication and division

3.5A.desc: Represent one- and two-step problems using symbolic representations

3.5B.desc: Describe a multiplication expression using related division facts

3.5C.desc: Describe a division expression using related multiplication facts

3.5D.desc: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers

3.6A.desc: Classify and sort two-dimensional figures, including circles, triangles, rectangles, squares, and rhombuses

3.6B.desc: Use attributes to describe how 2-D figures are alike and different

3.6C.desc: Compose two-dimensional shapes and three-dimensional solids with given properties or attributes

3.7A.desc: Measure lengths using standard units such as centimeters and inches

3.7B.desc: Determine the perimeter of a polygon or a missing length when given perimeter

3.8A.desc: Summarize a variety of data and represent the data on a frequency table, dot plot, pictograph, or bar graph

3.8B.desc: Determine the mode and range of a data set

4.2A.desc: Interpret the value of each place-value position as 10 times the position to the right

4.2B.desc: Describe the relationship between two numbers when they differ by a multiple of 10

4.2C.desc: Read and write whole numbers up to 1,000,000 using numerals, number words, and expanded form

4.2D.desc: Compare and order whole numbers up to 1,000,000 and represent comparisons using symbols

4.2E.desc: Represent the value of the digit in decimals through the hundredths using expanded notation and area models

4.2F.desc: Compare and order decimal numbers to the hundredths

4.2G.desc: Round whole numbers to a given place value through the hundred thousands place

4.2H.desc: Round decimal numbers to the nearest tenth or hundredth

4.3A.desc: Represent a fraction a/b as a sum of fractions 1/b, where a and b are whole numbers

4.3B.desc: Decompose a fraction in more than one way into a sum of fractions with the same denominator

4.3C.desc: Determine if two given fractions are equivalent using a variety of methods

4.3D.desc: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators

4.3E.desc: Represent and solve addition and subtraction of fractions with equal denominators

4.3F.desc: Represent fractions and decimals to the hundredths as distances from zero on a number line

4.3G.desc: Identify concrete models that represent equivalent fractions with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8

4.4A.desc: Add and subtract whole numbers and decimals to the hundredths place using standard algorithms

4.4B.desc: Determine products of a number and 10 or 100 using properties of operations

4.4C.desc: Solve word problems involving multiplication and division

4.4D.desc: Use strategies and algorithms, including the standard algorithm, to multiply up to a four-digit number by a one-digit number

4.4E.desc: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100

4.4F.desc: Multiply and divide to solve word problems involving whole numbers

4.4G.desc: Round to the nearest ten, hundred, or thousand

4.4H.desc: Solve with fluency one- and two-step problems involving multiplication using strategies

4.5A.desc: Represent multi-step problems involving the four operations using strip diagrams and equations

4.5B.desc: Represent problems using an input-output table and numerical expressions

4.5C.desc: Describe the meaning of parentheses and brackets in numeric expressions

4.5D.desc: Simplify numerical expressions that do not involve exponents using order of operations

4.6A.desc: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines

4.6B.desc: Classify and sort two- and three-dimensional figures based on attributes

4.6C.desc: Support generalizations about geometric properties and relationships using concrete models and drawings

4.6D.desc: Identify lines of symmetry in two-dimensional shapes

4.7A.desc: Measure angles in two-dimensional figures using a protractor

4.7B.desc: Illustrate that the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths is measured in square units

4.8A.desc: Identify relative sizes of measurement units within the customary and metric systems

4.8B.desc: Convert measurements within the same measurement system

4.8C.desc: Solve problems that deal with measurements of length, intervals of time, liquid volumes, mass, and money

4.9A.desc: Summarize a variety of data and represent the data on a frequency table, dot plot, pictograph, or bar graph

4.9B.desc: Solve one- and two-step problems using data from a frequency table, dot plot, pictograph, or bar graph

5.2A.desc: Represent the value of the digit in decimals through the thousandths using expanded notation and area models

5.2B.desc: Compare and order two decimals to thousandths and represent comparisons using symbols

5.2C.desc: Round decimals to the nearest tenth or hundredth

5.3A.desc: Estimate to determine solutions to mathematical and real-world problems involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division

5.3B.desc: Multiply with fluency a three-digit number by a two-digit number using standard algorithms

5.3C.desc: Solve with fluency one- and two-step problems involving multiplication and division

5.3D.desc: Identify and represent on a number line decimals, fractions, and mixed numbers

5.3E.desc: Solve for products of decimals to the hundredths

5.3F.desc: Represent quotients of decimals to the hundredths

5.3G.desc: Solve one-step and multi-step problems involving multiplication and division, including interpreting remainders

5.3H.desc: Represent and solve addition and subtraction of fractions with unequal denominators

5.3I.desc: Represent multiplication of two fractions or whole number and a fraction with area and length models

5.3J.desc: Find the product of a whole number and a fraction and represent using area models

5.3K.desc: Add and subtract positive rational numbers fluently

5.3L.desc: Divide whole numbers by unit fractions and unit fractions by whole numbers

5.4B.desc: Represent and solve multi-step problems involving the four operations with whole numbers

5.4C.desc: Use the order of operations to evaluate numerical expressions

5.4D.desc: Describe the meaning of parentheses and brackets in a numeric expression

5.4E.desc: Simplify numerical expressions that do not involve exponents using order of operations

5.4F.desc: Simplify numerical expressions that do not involve exponents using order of operations

5.5A.desc: Identify the attributes of triangles and classify them by their attributes or properties

5.6A.desc: Identify points, lines, line segments, rays, angles, and perpendicular and parallel lines

5.6B.desc: Identify and classify polygons based on their attributes

5.7A.desc: Describe the formula for the area of a rectangle as length times width

5.8A.desc: Describe the key attributes of the coordinate plane and the process of graphing ordered pairs

5.8B.desc: Describe the process for graphing ordered pairs of numbers in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane

5.8C.desc: Graph in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane ordered pairs of numbers arising from mathematical and real-world problems

5.9A.desc: Represent categorical data with bar graphs or frequency tables and numerical data with dot plots or stem-and-leaf plots

5.9B.desc: Represent data using tables and graphs such as line plots, line graphs, and stem-and-leaf plots

5.9C.desc: Solve one- and two-step problems using data from a frequency table, dot plot, bar graph, stem-and-leaf plot, or scatterplot

6.2A.desc: Classify whole numbers, integers, and rational numbers using a visual representation

6.2B.desc: Identify a number, its opposite, and its absolute value

6.2C.desc: Locate, compare, and order integers and rational numbers on a number line

6.2D.desc: Order a set of rational numbers arising from mathematical and real-world contexts

6.2E.desc: Extend representations for division to include fraction notation

6.3A.desc: Recognize that dividing by a rational number and multiplying by its reciprocal result in equivalent values

6.3B.desc: Determine, with and without computation, whether a quantity is increased or decreased by a fraction, decimal, or percent

6.3C.desc: Represent integer operations with concrete models and connect the actions to algorithms

6.3D.desc: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide integers fluently

6.3E.desc: Multiply and divide positive rational numbers fluently

6.4A.desc: Compare two rules verbally, numerically, symbolically, and graphically in the form of y=kx

6.4B.desc: Apply qualitative and quantitative reasoning to solve prediction and comparison problems

6.4C.desc: Give examples of ratios as multiplicative comparisons of two quantities

6.4D.desc: Give examples of rates as the comparison by division of two quantities having different attributes

6.4E.desc: Represent ratios and percents with concrete models, fractions, and decimals

6.4F.desc: Represent benchmark fractions and percents such as 1%, 10%, 25%, 33-1/3%, and multiples of these values

6.4G.desc: Generate equivalent forms of fractions, decimals, and percents using real-world problems

6.4H.desc: Convert units within a measurement system using proportions and unit rates

6.5A.desc: Represent mathematical and real-world problems involving ratios and rates using scale factors, tables, graphs, and proportions

6.5B.desc: Solve real-world problems to find the whole given a part and the percent

6.5C.desc: Use equivalent fractions, decimals, and percents to show equal parts of the same whole

6.6A.desc: Identify independent and dependent quantities from tables and graphs

6.6B.desc: Write an equation that represents the relationship between independent and dependent quantities

6.6C.desc: Represent a given situation using verbal descriptions, tables, graphs, and equations

6.7A.desc: Generate equivalent numerical expressions using order of operations

6.7B.desc: Distinguish between expressions and equations verbally, numerically, and algebraically

6.7C.desc: Determine if two expressions are equivalent using concrete models, pictorial models, and algebraic representations

6.7D.desc: Generate equivalent expressions using the properties of operations: inverse, identity, commutative, associative, and distributive

6.8A.desc: Extend previous knowledge of triangles and their properties to include the sum of angles

6.8B.desc: Model area formulas for parallelograms, trapezoids, and triangles

6.8C.desc: Write equations that represent problems related to the area of rectangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, and triangles

6.8D.desc: Determine solutions for problems involving the area of rectangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, and triangles

6.9A.desc: Write one-variable, one-step equations and inequalities to represent constraints or conditions within problems

6.9B.desc: Represent solutions for one-variable, one-step equations and inequalities on number lines

6.10A.desc: Model and solve one-variable, one-step equations and inequalities that represent problems

6.10B.desc: Determine if the given value(s) make(s) one-variable, one-step equations or inequalities true

6.11A.desc: Graph points in all four quadrants using ordered pairs of rational numbers

6.12A.desc: Represent numeric data graphically, including dot plots, stem-and-leaf plots, histograms, and box plots

6.12B.desc: Use the graphical representation of numeric data to describe the center, spread, and shape of the data distribution

6.12C.desc: Summarize numeric data with numerical summaries: the mean and median; the range and interquartile range

6.12D.desc: Summarize categorical data with numerical and graphical summaries using the mode, the percent of data in each category

6.13A.desc: Interpret numeric data summarized in dot plots, stem-and-leaf plots, histograms, and box plots

7.2A.desc: Extend previous knowledge of sets and subsets using a visual representation to describe relationships between sets

7.3A.desc: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers fluently

7.3B.desc: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations to solve problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of rational numbers

7.4A.desc: Represent constant rates of change in mathematical and real-world problems given pictorial, tabular, verbal, numeric, graphical, and algebraic representations

7.4B.desc: Calculate unit rates from rates in mathematical and real-world problems

7.4C.desc: Determine the constant of proportionality (k = y/x) within mathematical and real-world problems

7.4D.desc: Solve problems involving ratios, rates, and percents, including multi-step problems involving percent increase and percent decrease

7.4E.desc: Convert between measurement systems, including the use of proportions and the use of unit rates

7.5A.desc: Generalize the critical attributes of similarity, including ratios within and between similar shapes

7.5B.desc: Describe π as the ratio of the circumference of a circle to its diameter

7.5C.desc: Solve mathematical and real-world problems involving similar shape and scale drawings

7.6A.desc: Represent sample spaces for simple and compound events using lists and tree diagrams

7.6B.desc: Select and use different simulations to represent simple and compound events with and without technology

7.6C.desc: Make predictions and determine solutions using experimental data for simple and compound events

7.6D.desc: Make predictions and determine solutions using theoretical probability for simple and compound events

7.6E.desc: Find the probabilities of a simple event and its complement and describe the relationship

7.6F.desc: Use data from a random sample to make inferences about a population

7.6G.desc: Solve problems using data represented in bar graphs, dot plots, and circle graphs

7.6H.desc: Solve problems using qualitative and quantitative predictions and comparisons from simple experiments

7.6I.desc: Determine experimental and theoretical probabilities related to simple and compound events using data and sample spaces

7.7A.desc: Represent linear relationships using verbal, tabular, algebraic, and graphical forms

7.7B.desc: Represent solutions to an inequality using a number line and determine whether a statement is true

7.7C.desc: Determine the constant of variation given a proportional relationship in y = kx form

7.8A.desc: Model the relationship between the volume of a rectangular prism and a rectangular pyramid having both equal bases and heights

7.8B.desc: Explain verbally and symbolically the relationship between the volume of a triangular prism and a triangular pyramid

7.8C.desc: Use models to determine the approximate formulas for the circumference and area of a circle

7.9A.desc: Solve problems involving the volume of rectangular prisms, triangular prisms, rectangular pyramids, and triangular pyramids

7.9B.desc: Determine the circumference and area of circles

7.9C.desc: Determine the area of composite figures containing combinations of rectangles, squares, parallelograms, trapezoids, triangles, semicircles, and quarter circles

7.9D.desc: Solve problems involving the lateral and total surface area of a rectangular prism, rectangular pyramid, triangular prism, and triangular pyramid

7.10A.desc: Write one-variable, two-step equations and inequalities to represent constraints or conditions within problems

7.10B.desc: Represent solutions for one-variable, two-step equations and inequalities on number lines

7.11A.desc: Model and solve one-variable, two-step equations and inequalities

7.11B.desc: Determine if the given value(s) make(s) one-variable, two-step equations or inequalities true

7.11C.desc: Write and solve equations using geometry concepts, including the sum of angles in a triangle, and angle relationships

7.12A.desc: Compare two groups of numeric data using comparative dot plots or box plots by comparing the shapes and centers

7.12B.desc: Use data from a random sample to make inferences about a population

7.12C.desc: Compare two populations based on data in random samples from these populations, including informal comparative inferences

8.2A.desc: Extend previous knowledge of sets and subsets using a visual representation to describe relationships between sets of real numbers

8.2B.desc: Approximate the value of an irrational number, including π and square roots of non-perfect squares

8.2C.desc: Convert between standard decimal notation and scientific notation

8.2D.desc: Order a set of real numbers arising from mathematical and real-world contexts

8.3A.desc: Generalize that the ratio of corresponding sides of similar shapes are proportional, including a shape and its dilation

8.3B.desc: Compare and contrast the attributes of a shape and its dilation(s) on a coordinate plane

8.3C.desc: Use an algebraic representation to explain the effect of a given positive rational scale factor applied to two-dimensional figures

8.4A.desc: Use similar right triangles to develop an understanding that slope, m, given as the rate comparing the change in y-values to the change in x-values

8.4B.desc: Graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the line

8.4C.desc: Use data from a table or graph to determine the rate of change or slope and y-intercept in mathematical and real-world problems

8.5A.desc: Represent linear proportional situations with tables, graphs, and equations in the form y=kx

8.5B.desc: Represent linear non-proportional situations with tables, graphs, and equations in the form y=mx+b, where b≠0

8.5C.desc: Contrast bivariate sets of data that suggest linear relationships with bivariate sets of data that do not suggest linear relationships

8.5D.desc: Use a trend line that approximates the linear relationship between bivariate sets of data to make predictions

8.5F.desc: Distinguish between proportional and non-proportional linear situations using tables, graphs, and equations

8.5G.desc: Identify functions using sets of ordered pairs, tables, mappings, and graphs

8.5H.desc: Identify examples of proportional and non-proportional functions that arise from mathematical and real-world problems

8.5I.desc: Write an equation in the form y=mx+b to model a linear relationship between two quantities using verbal, numerical, tabular, and graphical representations

8.6A.desc: Describe the volume formula V=Bh of a cylinder in terms of its base area and its height

8.6B.desc: Model the relationship between the volume of a cylinder and a cone having both equal bases and heights

8.6C.desc: Use models and diagrams to explain the Pythagorean theorem

8.7A.desc: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to determine the distance between two points in a coordinate plane

8.7B.desc: Use previous knowledge of surface area to make connections to the formulas for lateral and total surface area

8.7C.desc: Use the Pythagorean theorem and its converse to solve problems

8.8A.desc: Write one-variable equations or inequalities with variables on both sides that represent problems

8.8B.desc: Write a corresponding real-world problem when given a one-variable equation or inequality

8.8C.desc: Model and solve one-variable equations with variables on both sides of the equal sign

8.9A.desc: Identify and verify the values of x and y that simultaneously satisfy two linear equations in two variables

8.10A.desc: Generalize the properties of orientation and congruence of reflections, rotations, translations, and dilations

8.10B.desc: Differentiate between transformations that preserve congruence and those that do not

8.10C.desc: Explain the effect of translations, reflections over the x- or y-axis, and rotations limited to 90°, 180°, 270°, and 360°

8.10D.desc: Model the effect of dilations on linear and area measurements of two-dimensional figures

8.11A.desc: Solve problems using the Pythagorean theorem in two and three dimensions

A.2A.desc: Determine the domain and range of a linear function in mathematical problems; determine reasonable domain and range values

A.6A.desc: Determine the domain and range of quadratic functions; determine reasonable domain and range values

A.6B.desc: Write equations of quadratic functions given the vertex and another point

A.6C.desc: Write quadratic equations when given real solutions and graphs of the related functions

A.7A.desc: Graph quadratic functions on the coordinate plane using various methods

A.7C.desc: Determine the effects of parameter changes on quadratic functions

A.12A.desc: Decide whether relations represented verbally, tabularly, graphically, and symbolically define a function

A.12B.desc: Evaluate functions, expressed in function notation, given one or more elements in their domains

G.2A.desc: Determine the lengths of sides and measures of angles in a right triangle by applying the Pythagorean theorem

G.2B.desc: Determine the distance between two points on a coordinate plane using the distance formula

G.5A.desc: Investigate patterns to make conjectures about geometric relationships

G.5B.desc: Construct congruent segments, congruent angles, a segment bisector, an angle bisector, perpendicular lines, a perpendicular bisector, and a line parallel to a given line

G.10A.desc: Apply the definition of similarity in terms of a dilation to identify similar figures

G.11A.desc: Apply the Triangle Inequality theorem to determine if three lengths are the sides of a triangle
